instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Features and Benefits of Group Life Insurance
Group insurance refers to insurance products that are extended to a group of people. Both life insurance and health insurance are available in the group insurance form. Here we look at the nuances of group life insurance plans.
Benefits of Group insurance policies:
Group policies tend to be affordable; the premiums are lower than that of individual insurance policies.
Employees are mandated to provide insurance to their employees. Hence they opt for group insurance policies which offer reasonable coverage at decent premiums.
They are economical for the employer and also meet the requirement to increase the sense of loyalty and enhance productivity among employees.
From the perspective of the insurer, the risk in group insurance is considerably low as they offer insurance for pooled risk, a wide variety of individuals with a varied risk profiles.
Regardless of age, profession, position and social background, group insurance policies may be extended.
Types of Group life insurance policies:
In addition to the policy provided by the employer, there are other types of group insurance policies that are extended:
Gratuity: By subscribing to the Group gratuity policy, which falls within the umbrella of group life insurance plans, the employer will be able to build a corpus for employees completing a certain number of years of service in the company. They can be offered to employees who move out after having provided a reasonable number of years of service. Gratuity is a compulsory benefit that the employer is required to extend to the employees under the Gratuity act, 1972.
Superannuation: A group superannuation fund is created to provide a one-time amount to employees during retirement.
Term policy: This is the most atypical group life insurance policy offered. They offer the sum assured to the beneficiary upon the untimely death of the employee, who is the insured in this case.
Term life coverage: These policies are renewed on an annual basis. They offer life coverage for the insured. Group insurance term cover is allowed for all employees without having to undergo medical tests. Employers paying the premium on behalf of the employees can avail of tax benefits. However, if the premium is being paid by the employee, the premium amount can be availed as tax benefits under section 80C upto Rs. 1.5 lakhs a year.
Permanent life coverage: These policies offer coverage for the employees for their entire lifespan or the term that they are employed with the organisation, whichever is earlier. There are some additional clauses in permanent life coverage. In the event the employee quits the company, the cash value will be made available to the other employees. If you avail of a paid-up policy which extends the benefit of the term policy and whole life insurance, then the cost of the term policy will be borne by the company, and the rest of the payment should be borne by the employee. In the event the employee quits the company midway, the policy will be transferred to the individual's name, and they can continue the policy as individual life insurance.
Credit protection: In the event, that an employee who has borrowed credit defaults on his loan due to death or disability, Group insurance protection plans offer to bear the outstanding credit and bear the losses of the bank/lending institution.
Savings plan: Group savings plans offer the opportunity to build wealth over the long haul. They have the benefit of hedging against life risk seamlessly alongside the wealth-building feature.
Features of Group life insurance term plans:
There are many companies offering term life insurance plans. While their features may differ from insurer to insurer, the basic offerings may be similar overall.
Many insurers offer graded cover, where the benefits can be offered based on the rank or category of the employees.
Each category of term life plan can be customised to the collective needs of the group of employees for whom the group insurance is extended.
Term policies do not offer any maturity benefit. Only a death benefit is extended to the employees who are covered.
Additional riders may be availed based on the discretion of the employee. However, often the employer does not bear such additional costs.
The party paying the insurance premium can avail of tax benefits.
The typical age of entry is 18 years, and the exit age is stipulated at 60 years.
The policy term can range from 5 – 35 years.
Group insurance is extended to a minimum group size of 50 members.
A major drawback of group insurance plans is that these plans are often too basic. They are not customised to the needs of the individual. You may require to avail an additional term cover tailormade to your needs, considering that the coverage does not entirely align with your requirement. Sometimes, group life insurance covers are not extended to your children and spouse. Their specific insurance needs, such as coverage for continuation of education, home loan coverage etc., need to be addressed separately.
Group insurance is an efficient and economical way to extend benefits to employees. However, individual plans, in addition to group life insurance, will provide comprehensive and well-rounded coverage and should be taken alongside to enhance the coverage.

output.final_text:
Features and Benefits of Group Life Insurance Group insurance refers to insurance products that are extended to a group of people. Both life insurance and health insurance are available in the group insurance form. Here we look at the nuances of group life insurance plans. Benefits of Group insurance policies: 1. Group policies tend to be affordable; the premiums are lower than that of individual insurance
policies. 2. Employers are mandated to provide insurance to their employees subject to the provisions under
EDLI (Employees Deposit-Linked Insurance) scheme known as The Employees’ Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act, 1952 . 3. They are relatively affordable for the employer and may be considered as one of the ways to
increase the sense of loyalty and enhance productivity among employees. 4. For extending the term of group insurance policies, barring age restrictions, factors like profession,
position and social background are agreed during pricing discussions. In some cases, age may also be discussed, as may be deemed suitable to the insurer. Types of Group life insurance policies: Some of the other types of group insurance policies that are extended:
1. Gratuity1:
Gratuity is a mandatory benefit payable to the employees by the employer, as per the Gratuity Act, 1972. The lumpsum amount is payable to the employee, when they are no longer a part of the company. By subscribing to the Group gratuity policy, which falls within the umbrella of group life insurance plans, the employer will be able to build a corpus for employees completing atleast 5 years of service in the company. The maximum amount of gratuity payable under the Gratuity Act, 1972 is capped at INR 20 lakhs2, subject to the satisfaction of the terms and conditions under the Act. 2. Superannuation:
A group superannuation fund is created to provide a one-time amount to employees at the time of retirement. 3. Term policy:
This is the most common group life insurance policy offered. They offer the sum assured to the beneficiary upon the untimely death of the employee, who is the insured in this case. These policies are renewed on an annual basis. They offer life coverage for the insured. Group insurance term cover, under employer-employee scheme, is allowed for all employees who may or may not have to undergo medical tests. Employers paying the premium for employees can avail of tax benefits as per the provisions stated in the Income Tax Act, 1961. However, if the premium is being paid by the employee, the premium amount can be availed as tax benefits by the employee under section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961, up to Rs. 1.5 lakhs a year.
4. Credit protection:
Credit protection policy may be taken by a lender who becomes the Master Policy Holder and borrowers are covered under the master policy. In case of any unfortunate event with the borrower/ life assured (member), claim amount is adjusted towards outstanding loan & balance is paid to the nominee of the life assured (member). Features of Group life insurance term plans: There are many companies offering term life insurance plans. While their features may differ from insurer to insurer, the basic offerings may be similar overall.
1. Many insurers offer graded cover, where the benefits can be offered based on the rank or
category of the employees. 2. Each category of term life plan can be customised to the collective needs of the group of
employees for whom the group insurance is extended. 3. Term policies do not offer any maturity benefit. Only the death benefit is extended to the
employees who are covered, subject to the terms of the plan. 4. Additional riders may be availed, on payment of extra premium, subject to conditions set out by
the Insurer & the Employer. However, often the employer does not bear such additional costs. 5. The party paying the insurance premium can avail of tax benefits as per provisions stated in
Income Tax Act, 1961. 6. The typical age of entry is 18 years, and in general, exit age is 80 for Employer-Employee and 70
for Non Employer-Employee. It may vary from Insurer to Insurer. 7. Group insurance is extended to a minimum group size of 50 members. A major drawback of group insurance plans is that these plans cannot be customised to the needs of the individual, but of a group. You may require to avail an additional term cover tailormade to your needs, considering that the coverage does not entirely align with your requirement. Group life insurance covers only the members of the Group, e.g., in case of Employer-Employee relationships, only the employees can be covered. Corporates have an option to take a separate top-up policy for spouses if they so require. Their specific insurance needs, such as coverage for continuation of education, home loan coverage etc., need to be addressed separately. Group insurance is an efficient and economical way to extend benefits to employees. However, individual plans, in addition to group life insurance, will provide comprehensive and well-rounded coverage and should be considered to be taken alongside to enhance the coverage. Reference:
1 https://www.mondaq.com/india/employee-benefits--compensation/768716/key- amendments-in-payment-of-gratuity- act#:~:text=The%20amendment%20aims%20to%20double,Act%22)%2C%20is%20considered
2 https://labour.gov.in/sites/default/files/Press%20release-6%2003%202019%20final.pdf
